# when-code-when-llm

> A Claude Code skill (MIT) that decides, per task, whether to write deterministic code (regex, schema validation, AST parse) or call an LLM (classification, judge, embeddings). One axis — structural vs semantic — plus a false-positive test and an enumerate/decide split for mixed tasks.

## Core documentation

- [llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt): self-contained Q&A reference for AI agents (Project Facts, Prior Research, question-form H2 sections)
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing narrative (install, decision axis table, trigger conditions)
- [SKILL.md](skills/when-code-when-llm/SKILL.md): full skill specification — structural-vs-semantic axis, false-positive test, worked examples in both failure directions, enumerate/decide split, 6-item decision checklist

## Architecture

- Single judgment axis: **structural** (decidable from bytes — format, presence, schema) → code; **semantic** (requires meaning — intent, category, similarity) → LLM
- False-positive test: "can two inputs make the same rule right for one and wrong for the other, where the difference is meaning, not characters?" — yes means semantic
- Two symmetric failure modes: code-where-LLM-belongs (regex sprouting exceptions) and LLM-where-code-belongs (paying tokens for `str.endswith`)
- Enumerate/decide split: when one task mixes both, code enumerates deterministically (linter in CI), judgment resolves (review conversation decides) — never let one tool do both halves
- Canonical copy lives in the author's Claude Code harness; this repo is a one-way mirror via `scripts/sync-from-local.sh`

## Standard reference

- [Agent Skill specification](https://agentskills.io/specification): the skill format used by this repo
- [AKC ADR-0008 "Code-LLM Layering"](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle/blob/main/docs/adr/0008-code-and-llm-collaboration.md): the "why" this skill is the "how" of — code owns determinism and termination, LLM owns meaning

## Sibling skills

- [code-and-llm-collaboration](https://github.com/shimo4228/code-and-llm-collaboration): the same structural-vs-semantic axis at pipeline level — how code layers and LLM layers compose
- [search-first](https://github.com/shimo4228/search-first): enforces research-before-coding with a 4-way Adopt / Extend / Compose / Build decision
- [llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/llms-txt-writer): writes AI-facing docs optimized for ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini citation
- [skill-comply](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-comply): measures whether agents actually follow skill / rule definitions
- [context-sync](https://github.com/shimo4228/context-sync): audits and fixes project documentation roles
- [skill-stocktake](https://github.com/shimo4228/skill-stocktake): quality audit for skills and commands
- [rules-distill](https://github.com/shimo4228/rules-distill): scan installed skills, extract cross-cutting principles, and distill them into rules
- [learn-eval](https://github.com/shimo4228/learn-eval): extract reusable patterns from sessions, self-evaluate quality, and save to the right location
